title: INSERT with Constraint Handling
description: Demonstrates INSERT operation and handling potential constraint violations
code:
-- Basic INSERT
INSERT INTO employees (emp_id, name, dept_id, salary)
VALUES (101, 'John Smith', 10, 50000);

-- INSERT with CHECK constraint violation example
-- This will fail if salary < 30000 due to CHECK constraint
INSERT INTO employees (emp_id, name, dept_id, salary)
VALUES (102, 'Jane Doe', 20, 25000); -- Violates CHECK salary >= 30000
explanation: INSERT adds new rows. If any constraint (NOT NULL, UNIQUE, PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, CHECK) is violated, the operation fails and is rolled back.

complexity.time: O(n) where n is number of indexes

title: Transaction with Savepoints
description: Demonstrates transaction control with COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and SAVEPOINT
explanation: Transaction groups multiple operations. SAVEPOINT allows partial rollback without losing entire transaction work.

title: Two-Phase Locking Example
description: Demonstrates growing and shrinking phases in 2PL protocol
code:
-- Transaction T1: Two-Phase Locking
BEGIN;

-- Growing Phase (acquire locks)
SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE acc_id = 1 LOCK IN SHARE MODE; -- Shared Lock
UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 500 WHERE acc_id = 1; -- Escalate to Exclusive Lock

-- Lock Point: Maximum locks acquired here

-- Shrinking Phase (release locks)
COMMIT; -- Releases all locks

-- Cannot acquire new locks after this point in same transaction
explanation: 2PL ensures serializability by not acquiring new locks after starting to release locks. The growing phase acquires all needed locks, shrinking phase releases them.

title: DELETE with Foreign Key Cascade
description: Shows DELETE operation with referential integrity actions
explanation: ON DELETE CASCADE automatically removes child rows when parent is deleted, maintaining referential integrity.

complexity.time: O(n + m) where n=parent rows, m=child rows

title: Handling Constraint Violations with ON CONFLICT
description: Demonstrates UPSERT pattern to handle PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE violations
code:
-- Insert with conflict handling (PostgreSQL)
INSERT INTO products (product_id, name, price)
VALUES (101, 'Laptop', 50000)
ON CONFLICT (product_id) 
DO UPDATE SET price = EXCLUDED.price, 
 name = EXCLUDED.name;

-- Alternative: Do nothing on conflict
INSERT INTO products (product_id, name, price)
VALUES (101, 'Laptop', 50000)
ON CONFLICT (product_id) 
DO NOTHING;
explanation: ON CONFLICT (UPSERT) handles PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE violations by updating existing row or ignoring the operation instead of failing.

complexity.time: O(log n) for index search + O(n) for update
